steps.title: Start with the relationship
steps.content:
The given relationship is
$$
11m=9(n+p)
$$
The goal is to isolate $m$.

steps.title: Divide by the coefficient
steps.content: Since $m$ is multiplied by $11$, divide both sides by $11$. $$ \frac{11m}{11}=\frac{9(n+p)}{11} $$ $$ m=\frac{9(n+p)}{11} $$ The answer is A.
